  • The chart shows 'Sell' signals appearing in April around the $320-$330 price range, indicating potential short-term weakness or consolidation.
  • The Relative Strength Index (RSI) is currently at 63.90, which is in the bullish zone but not oversold, suggesting the price may be extended.
  • The RSI purple line has recently crossed above the orange signal line, but this crossover is not occurring from an oversold condition, which would typically indicate a stronger buy signal.

News & Financials

  • VRT is currently in a strong long-term uptrend, having recently made new all-time highs.
  • The chart shows 'Sell' signals appearing in April around the $320-$330 price range, indicating potential short-term weakness or consolidation.
  • The Relative Strength Index (RSI) is currently at 63.90, which is in the bullish zone but not oversold, suggesting the price may be extended.
  • The RSI purple line has recently crossed above the orange signal line, but this crossover is not occurring from an oversold condition, which would typically indicate a stronger buy signal.

Assessment

  • Buy level: Wait for a significant pullback to established support levels (e.g., around $280-$300) or for the RSI to dip into oversold territory and then show a bullish crossover.
  • Risk: High risk for initiating a new position at current elevated price levels, especially after recent 'Sell' signals and a slight pullback from all-time highs.
  • Action: Given the current 0.0% portfolio allocation, it is recommended to wait for a more favorable entry point, such as a deeper pullback to support or a clearer bullish signal from the RSI in an oversold region, before considering a starter position.

VWO VWO (~58.99) WAIT

Price: $58.99 | Entry Zone: $54–56

Technicals

  • The stock is in an established uptrend since late 2025.
  • The Relative Strength Index (RSI) purple line is currently above its orange signal line, indicating bullish momentum.
  • The RSI value is approximately 62.85, suggesting the stock is not yet overbought.
  • A 'Buy' signal dot appeared in late April when the price was around $53-$54, followed by a significant upward move.
  • The current price of $58.99 is near recent highs, approaching the 'Post' price of $59.06.

News & Financials

  • The stock is in an established uptrend since late 2025.
  • The Relative Strength Index (RSI) purple line is currently above its orange signal line, indicating bullish momentum.
  • The RSI value is approximately 62.85, suggesting the stock is not yet overbought.
  • A 'Buy' signal dot appeared in late April when the price was around $53-$54, followed by a significant upward move.
  • The current price of $58.99 is near recent highs, approaching the 'Post' price of $59.06.

Assessment

  • Buy level: A more favorable buy level would be on a pullback towards the previous support zone of $54-$56.
  • Risk: Moderate risk for adding to the position at current levels, as the price is near recent highs and has moved significantly since the last 'Buy' signal. The existing 10.4% allocation is already substantial.
  • Action: Hold the existing position. Wait for a clear pullback to the $54-$56 range or a confirmed breakout above $60 before considering any additional purchases.

html:
VXUS Vanguard Total International Stock ETF (~82.97) WAIT

⇗ Top

Price: $82.97 | Entry Zone: $76.00–78.00

Technicals

  • Price is currently near recent highs, showing signs of consolidation after a strong uptrend.
  • RSI is neutral at 59.29, with the purple line trending slightly downward toward the orange signal line, suggesting waning momentum.
  • Recent 'Sell' signal dots on the chart indicate potential short-term exhaustion.
  • The asset is trading well above its established diagonal trendline support.

News & Financials

  • Price is currently near recent highs, showing signs of consolidation after a strong uptrend.
  • RSI is neutral at 59.29, with the purple line trending slightly downward toward the orange signal line, suggesting waning momentum.
  • Recent 'Sell' signal dots on the chart indicate potential short-term exhaustion.
  • The asset is trading well above its established diagonal trendline support.

Assessment

  • Buy level: Look for a pullback toward the 76.00-78.00 range where the trendline support aligns with previous consolidation zones.
  • Risk: Moderate; the asset is currently extended from its primary trendline, increasing the risk of a mean-reversion pullback.
  • Action: Hold current position. Given the 3.1% allocation is already above the starter threshold, avoid adding at current highs. Consider trimming 25% (~37.5 shares) if the RSI purple line crosses below the orange line decisively to lock in gains.
